name: Indonesian Coconut Beef
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the trimmed sirloin steak into half-inch thick strips.
2. Heat corn oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
3. Add sliced onions and garlic; fry gently until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Add beef to the pan and stir-fry until browned on all sides, about 10-12 minutes.
5. Stir in ground ginger, cumin, coriander, and chili seasoning; cook for 2 minutes.
6. Add shredded coconut, brown sugar, lemon juice, and beef stock. Stir well to combine.
7. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, uncovered, over medium-low heat.
8. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 30-35 minutes, or until the mixture thickens and becomes dry.
9. Stir more frequently towards the end of cooking time to prevent sticking.
10. Garnish with slivers of red bell pepper, green chilies, and small slices of onion before serving.
NOTE: If you prefer a more moist mixture, reduce cooking time to 20-25 minutes instead of 30-35 minutes.
